differentials.name: Unilateral facet lock
differentials.raw: Unilateral facet lock (oblique views!, stable fracture)

differentials.name: Burst fracture = intervertebral disk driven into vertebral body below
differentials.raw: Burst fracture = intervertebral disk driven into vertebral body below (fracture may be stable / unstable)

Unilateral facet lock: "bow-tie" sign = the 4 rotated facets on LAT view
Jefferson fracture: sagittal fracture component extending to inferior endplate
posterior element fracture Associated with:   widening of apophyseal joints, fracture of posterior vertebral arches
Burst fracture = intervertebral disk dri: loss of posterior vertebral body height with several fragments:
Transverse process fracture [Lateral ver: rotation of spinous process (on AP view)

context: √  anterolisthesis < ¼ vertebral body width √  "bow-tie" sign = the 4 rotated facets on LAT view √  decrease in spinolaminar space √  rotation of spinous process (on AP view) √  "naked facet" (on CT) =  axial loading √  loss of posterior vertebral body height with several fragments: √  sagittal fracture component extending to inferior endplate √  retropulsed fragment from posterior superior margin in spinal canal √  interpedicular widening √  posterior element fracture Associated with:   widening of apophyseal joints, fracture of posterior vertebral arches
